title: diphasium lindseaceum
intro: Diphasium lindseae is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae, commonly known as the Lindley's forked fern or the forked water-fern, that is native to the subtropical regions of Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent effects. The health benefits of Diphasium lindseae include its ability to treat various conditions such as eczema, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. Diphasium lindseae can be prepared into herbal remedies like infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically or consumed as a tea to harness its medicinal properties.
